title: Escape Characters
content:
### Escape Characters

**Escape characters** let you insert special characters into strings using a backslash `\`.

---

### Common Escape Sequences

| Sequence | Name | Example | Result |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| `\'` | Single quote | `'It\'s fine'` | `It's fine` |
| `\"` | Double quote | `"He said \"Hi\""` | `He said "Hi"` |
| `\\` | Backslash | `"path\\file"` | `path\file` |
| `\n` | New line | `"Line1\nLine2"` | Line1, new line, Line2 |
| `\t` | Tab | `"Col1\tCol2"` | Col1 (tab) Col2 |
| `\r` | Carriage return | `"Hello\rWorld"` | `World` |
| `\b` | Backspace | `"Hello\b!"` | `Hell!` |
| `\f` | Form feed | Used in printers | Page break |
| `\ooo` | Octal value | `"\110\145"` | `He` |
| `\xhh` | Hex value | `"\x48\x65"` | `He` |

---

### New Line and Tab

```python
print("Line 1\nLine 2")
print("Column 1\tColumn 2")
```

---

### Raw Strings

Prefix with `r` to treat backslashes as literal:

```python
print("C:\\Users\\Name")       # Requires escaping
print(r"C:\Users\Name")       # Raw string - no escaping!
```

---

> **Use Case:** Raw strings are especially useful for regex patterns and file paths on Windows.
codeExample:
# Escape examples
print("Line 1\nLine 2")
print("Column 1\tColumn 2")
print("She said \"Hello!\"")
print("It\'s a nice day")

# Raw string
path = r"C:\Users\Name"
print(path)

quiz.question: What escape sequence creates a new line?
